content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please describe a moment in the Balancing Tower Game when you felt the most nervous. How did you keep yourself calm or focused?
回答問題：
請描述一次你在瑪利歐驚險塔遊戲中感到最緊張的時刻，你是如何讓自己保持冷靜或專注的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When the tower was leaning heavily on my turn, I felt extremely nervous that it would collapse. To keep myself calm, I took a deep breath and focused entirely on the placement of my character. I ignored the distracting comments from my friends, which helped me carefully balance the piece without shaking.|I was most nervous when I had to place Bowser on top of the already shaky tower. My hands were literally trembling. I managed to stay focused by visualizing the exact spot I wanted to place him before making a move. Being deliberate and slow helped me control my anxiety.|Placing my figure on a tilted platform made me incredibly tense. I felt my heart racing because I didn't want to be the one to lose. To maintain focus, I stopped talking, steadied my hand by resting it on the table for a second, and gently released the figure. This physical grounding really calmed my nerves.
9	When the tower was leaning on my turn, I felt very nervous it would collapse. To keep calm, I took a deep breath and focused on my character. I ignored comments from friends, which helped me balance the piece.|I was most nervous when placing a character on the shaky tower. My hands were trembling. I stayed focused by visualizing the spot before making a move. Being slow helped me control my anxiety.|Placing my figure on a tilted platform made me tense. I felt my heart racing. To maintain focus, I stopped talking, steadied my hand on the table, and released the figure. This calmed my nerves.
8	When the tower lean on my turn, I feel very nervous. To keep calm, I take a deep breath and focus on my character. I ignore my friends talk, it help me balance.|I am most nervous when I put character on shaky tower. My hand tremble. I stay focus by looking at the spot. Be slow help me control anxiety.|Put figure on tilted platform make me tense. Heart race. I stop talk, steady my hand, and put figure. This calm me.
7	When tower lean, I feel nervous. I take deep breath to keep calm. I focus my character and not listen friend.|I nervous when put character on shaky tower. Hand shake. I look spot and be slow. It control anxiety.|Put figure on tower make me tense. Heart fast. I stop talk and steady hand. It make me calm.
6	Tower lean, I nervous. Take breath keep calm. Focus character not listen friend.|Nervous put character on tower. Hand shake. Look spot be slow. Control anxiety.|Put figure make tense. Heart fast. Stop talk steady hand. Make calm.
5	Tower lean nervous. Take breath. Focus character.|Nervous put character. Hand shake. Look spot.|Figure tense. Heart fast. Stop talk.
4	Tower nervous. Breath. Focus.|Nervous. Hand shake. Look.|Tense. Heart. Stop.
3	Tower nervous. Breath.|Nervous. Hand.|Tense. Heart.
2	Nervous. Breath.|Nervous.|Tense.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please share a recent real-life experience where you had to make a quick decision under pressure (e.g., when discussing a final group report, needing to decide quickly within the teacher's deadline but failing to reach a consensus). How did you adjust your mood or cope with it?
回答問題：
請分享生活中最近一次你在壓力下需要迅速做決定的經驗(例如討論小組期末報告時，在老師訂的期限內需快速決定，但無法達成共識時)，你是如何調整心情或應對的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	Recently, our study group couldn't agree on a final topic, and the teacher's deadline was rapidly approaching. I felt incredibly pressured and anxious. To cope with my mood, I took a deep breath, paused the argument, and suggested a quick blind vote. This fast decision allowed us to reach a consensus and calmed everyone down.|I had to make a quick decision during a basketball game when my team was losing in the final minutes. I was very stressed. I coped with it by taking a deep breath and focusing on my coach's instructions instead of the scoreboard. We executed a fast play, and although we still lost, I felt good about my calm reaction.|Last week, I accidentally spilled water on my laptop right before an online class. I panicked but had to act fast. I immediately turned it off, wiped it dry, and grabbed my tablet to join the class. I adjusted my mood by accepting the accident and focusing on finding an immediate solution rather than blaming myself.
9	Recently, our study group couldn't agree on a topic, and the deadline was rapidly approaching. I felt pressured and anxious. To cope, I took a deep breath, paused the argument, and suggested a quick blind vote. This fast decision allowed us to reach a consensus and calmed everyone.|I had to make a quick decision during a basketball game when my team was losing. I was stressed. I coped by taking a deep breath and focusing on instructions. We executed a fast play, and I felt good about my calm reaction.|I accidentally spilled water on my laptop before an online class. I panicked but acted fast. I turned it off and grabbed my tablet to join the class. I adjusted my mood by focusing on finding a solution rather than blaming myself.
8	My group could not agree on a topic and the deadline was close. I feel pressure and anxious. To cope with my mood, I take deep breath and suggest a quick vote. This fast decision help us reach consensus and calm everyone down.|I make quick decision in basketball game when we lose. I am stress. I cope by taking deep breath and focus on coach. We play fast and I feel calm.|I spill water on laptop before class. I panic but act fast. I turn off and use tablet. I adjust mood by focus on solution and not blame myself.
7	My group cannot agree on topic and deadline is close. I feel pressure. I take deep breath to adjust mood. I suggest quick vote to make decision. It calm everyone and we agree.|I decide quick in basketball game when losing. I feel stress. I take breath and listen coach. We play and I am calm.|I spill water on computer before class. I act fast. I turn off computer and use tablet. I focus on solve problem.
6	Group cannot agree topic deadline close. Feel pressure. Take deep breath adjust mood. Suggest quick vote make decision. Calm everyone agree.|Decide quick in basketball. Feel stress. Take breath listen coach. Play fast and calm.|Spill water on computer. Act fast turn off. Use tablet to solve problem.
5	Group no agree topic deadline close. Pressure. Take breath. Suggest vote. Calm everyone.|Quick decision basketball. Stress. Take breath listen. Calm.|Spill water computer. Act fast turn off. Solve problem.
4	Group no agree topic. Deadline close. Pressure. Vote.|Quick basketball. Stress. Calm.|Spill water. Act fast. Solve.
3	Group no agree. Pressure. Vote.|Basketball. Stress.|Spill water. Fast.
2	Pressure. Vote.|Stress.|Fast.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
When facing pressure, what do you feel you need to improve the most? (Emotion management, focus, patience, time management, etc.) Why?
回答問題：
在面對壓力時，你覺得自己最需要改進的是什麼？(情緒管理、專注力、耐心、時間掌控等) 為什麼？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When facing pressure, I feel I need to improve my emotion management the most. Often, I let anxiety take over, which clouds my judgment and makes me act impulsively. If I can learn to stay calm and positive, I will be able to handle unexpected situations much more effectively.|I definitely need to improve my time management when I am under pressure. I tend to spend too much time worrying about the problem instead of breaking it down into manageable steps. Better time management would reduce my stress and help me complete tasks efficiently before deadlines.|Focus is the area I need to strengthen the most under pressure. I easily get distracted by people around me or negative thoughts when things go wrong. Training myself to block out distractions and concentrate solely on the task at hand would greatly improve my performance.
9	When facing pressure, I feel I need to improve my emotion management the most. I let anxiety take over, which clouds my judgment and makes me act impulsively. If I learn to stay calm, I will handle unexpected situations more effectively.|I need to improve my time management when under pressure. I spend too much time worrying instead of breaking problems into manageable steps. Better time management would reduce my stress and help me complete tasks.|Focus is the area I need to strengthen under pressure. I easily get distracted by people or negative thoughts. Training myself to block distractions and concentrate would greatly improve my performance.
8	When facing pressure, I need to improve emotion management most. I let anxiety take over and it make my judgment bad. If I stay calm, I can handle situations better.|I need to improve time management when under pressure. I worry too much and do not solve the problem step by step. Good time management will reduce stress and help me finish work.|Focus is the area I need to improve under pressure. I get distracted by people or bad thoughts. If I learn to block distractions, I will do much better.
7	When face pressure, I need improve emotion management. Anxiety make me judge bad. If I am calm, I handle things better.|I should improve time management under pressure. I worry too much and not solve problem. Time management make me less stress.|I need improve focus under pressure. People and bad thoughts distract me. I want learn to block them and do better.
6	Face pressure, need improve emotion. Anxiety make bad judge. Calm make things better.|Improve time management under pressure. Worry too much not solve. It make less stress.|Need improve focus under pressure. Distracted by people. Want block them do better.
5	Improve emotion under pressure. Anxiety bad. Calm better.|Improve time management. Worry much. Less stress.|Improve focus. Distracted people. Block them.
4	Improve emotion. Anxiety. Calm.|Improve time. Worry. Stress.|Improve focus. Distract.
3	Emotion. Calm.|Time. Stress.|Focus. Distract.
2	Emotion.|Time.|Focus.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
How did you react when a mistake made the tower shake or fall? Did you feel frustrated or did you laugh it off?
回答問題：
當失誤導致塔搖晃或倒塌時，你有什麼反應？你是感到挫折還是笑笑帶過？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When a mistake caused the tower to fall, I initially felt a brief moment of frustration because I was trying so hard to win. However, seeing the characters fly everywhere was quite funny, so I just laughed it off with my friends. It reminded me that the primary goal of the game is to have fun together, not just to win.|I accidentally knocked the tower over on my turn, and I felt a bit embarrassed at first. My friends started teasing me playfully, and instead of getting upset, I decided to laugh along with them. Owning my mistake and turning it into a joke completely removed any tension from the room.|When another player made the tower collapse, they looked really disappointed. I reacted by smiling and telling them it was completely fine, as the tower was already super unstable. We all cheered for the spectacular crash, which made everyone feel relaxed and ready for the next round.
9	When a mistake caused the tower to fall, I felt a brief moment of frustration because I was trying to win. However, seeing the characters fly was funny, so I laughed it off with my friends. It reminded me that the goal is to have fun together.|I accidentally knocked the tower over, and I felt embarrassed at first. My friends started teasing me, and instead of getting upset, I laughed along with them. Owning my mistake turned it into a joke and removed any tension.|When another player made the tower collapse, they looked disappointed. I reacted by telling them it was fine, as the tower was unstable. We cheered for the crash, which made everyone relaxed.
8	When mistake cause tower to fall, I feel little frustration because I try to win. But seeing characters fly is funny, so I laugh with friends. It remind me game is for fun.|I accidentally knock tower over and feel embarrassed. My friends tease me playfully, and I do not get upset. I laugh with them and it remove tension.|When other player make tower collapse, they look sad. I tell them it is fine because tower is unstable. We cheer for crash and everyone feel relax.
7	When mistake make tower fall, I feel frustrate because I want win. But it is funny to see, so I laugh with friends. Game is for fun.|I make tower fall and feel embarrass. Friends tease me but I not angry. I laugh with them and feel good.|When player drop tower, they sad. I smile say it is fine. We cheer and feel relax for next round.
6	Mistake make tower fall, I feel frustrate. But it funny, I laugh with friends. Game is fun.|I make tower fall and embarrass. Friends tease I not angry. Laugh with them.|Player drop tower they sad. I say fine. We cheer feel relax.
5	Mistake tower fall. Frustrate but funny. Laugh with friends.|Tower fall embarrass. Friends tease not angry. Laugh.|Tower fall sad. Say fine. Cheer relax.
4	Tower fall. Frustrate. Laugh.|Fall embarrass. Not angry. Laugh.|Fall sad. Say fine. Relax.
3	Fall. Laugh.|Embarrass. Laugh.|Sad. Fine.
2	Laugh.|Embarrass.|Sad.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
How did you communicate with or encourage other players when it was their turn and the tower was highly unstable?
回答問題：
當輪到其他玩家且塔非常不穩時，你是如何與他們溝通或鼓勵他們的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When it was my friend's turn and the tower was highly unstable, I could see they were very nervous. I encouraged them by speaking in a soft, calm voice, telling them to take their time and not to worry if it falls. This reassurance helped them relax and carefully place their piece without feeling pressured.|I communicated with a struggling player by pointing out a safe spot on the tower that they might have missed. I said, 'You can do this, just aim for the blue platform!' My enthusiastic encouragement gave them a confidence boost, and they successfully placed their figure without dropping the tower.|During a critical turn, everyone was silent and tense. I decided to break the ice by saying, 'No pressure, we are all ready for the explosion!' This lighthearted comment made the active player laugh, which eased their anxiety and allowed them to focus better on their move.
9	When it was my friend's turn and the tower was unstable, they were very nervous. I encouraged them by speaking in a calm voice, telling them to take their time. This reassurance helped them relax and place their piece without feeling pressured.|I communicated with a struggling player by pointing out a safe spot on the tower. I said, 'You can do this, just aim for the blue platform!' My encouragement gave them confidence, and they successfully placed their figure.|During a critical turn, everyone was tense. I broke the ice by saying, 'No pressure, we are ready for the explosion!' This lighthearted comment made the player laugh, which eased their anxiety and helped them focus.
8	When it is my friend turn and tower is unstable, they look nervous. I encourage them by speaking in calm voice and tell them to take time. This help them relax and put piece careful.|I communicate with player by point out a safe spot on tower. I say 'You can do it!' My encouragement give them confidence and they put figure success.|When everyone is tense, I say 'No pressure, we ready for explosion!' This funny comment make the player laugh and less anxious. They focus better on move.
7	When my friend turn and tower unstable, they very nervous. I encourage them with calm voice. I say take time. It make them relax and play good.|I help player by point safe spot on tower. I say you can do it. My encourage make them confident and they success.|Everyone very tense. I say no pressure. This make player laugh and not nervous. They focus better.
6	Friend turn tower unstable, they nervous. I encourage with calm voice. Say take time. Make them relax.|Help player point safe spot. Say you can do it. Make them confident success.|Everyone tense. Say no pressure. Make player laugh not nervous. Focus better.
5	Friend nervous. Encourage calm voice. Take time relax.|Point safe spot. Say you can do it. Confident success.|Tense. Say no pressure. Laugh not nervous. Focus.
4	Friend nervous. Encourage calm.|Point safe spot. Confident.|Tense. No pressure. Laugh.
3	Nervous. Encourage.|Safe spot. Confident.|Tense. Laugh.
2	Encourage.|Confident.|Laugh.
1	(No response / Silence)
